task: Investigate the association between the Composite Dietary Antioxidant Index (CDAI) and the prevalence of Parkinson's disease (PD) and all-cause mortality in PD patients among U.S. adults using NHANES 2007-2018 data with mortality follow-up. The analysis should: (1) calculate the Composite Dietary Antioxidant Index (CDAI) by summing standardized dietary intakes of six antioxidants — vitamins A, C, and E, carotenoids, selenium, and zinc — from 24-hour dietary recalls and supplement use data; (2) identify PD cases based on prescription of PD-specific medications (carbidopa, levodopa, entacapone, amantadine, pramipexole, bromocriptine); (3) estimate adjusted odds ratios for the association between CDAI tertiles and PD prevalence using survey-weighted multivariable logistic regression, adjusting for age, gender, hypertension, diabetes, fasting glucose, ALT, creatinine, triglycerides, and total cholesterol; (4) among PD patients, estimate adjusted hazard ratios for the association between CDAI and all-cause mortality using survey-weighted Cox proportional hazards regression linked to the National Death Index; (5) characterize the dose-response relationship between CDAI and PD prevalence using restricted cubic spline (RCS) regression to identify nonlinear patterns and threshold effects; (6) conduct subgroup analyses stratified by age (<60 vs >=60), gender, smoking status, hypertension, diabetes, and alcohol consumption to identify vulnerable populations. The study population includes U.S. adults aged 18 years and older from six pooled NHANES cycles (2007-2018) with complete dietary recall data, medication records, and mortality follow-up. All analyses must account for the complex survey design of NHANES using appropriate sampling weights, PSU, and strata.

data.name: NHANES 2007-2018 Demographics (6 cycles)
data.description: Demographic variables from DEMO_E through DEMO_J including age, sex, race/ethnicity, education, poverty income ratio. Combined across 6 cycles.

data.name: NHANES 2007-2018 Dietary Intake — 24-Hour Recall (6 cycles)
data.description: Total nutrient intakes from two 24-hour dietary recalls (DR1TOT and DR2TOT files) including vitamins A, C, E, carotenoids, selenium, zinc. Plus dietary supplement data (DSQTOT files).

data.name: NHANES 2007-2018 Prescription Medications (6 cycles)
data.description: Prescription medication data (RXQ_RX files) for identifying PD cases based on PD-specific medications: carbidopa, levodopa, entacapone, amantadine, pramipexole, bromocriptine.

data.name: NHANES 2007-2018 Medical History and Covariates (6 cycles)
data.description: Blood pressure questionnaire (BPQ), diabetes questionnaire (DIQ), standard biochemistry profile (BIOPRO for fasting glucose, ALT, creatinine, triglycerides, total cholesterol), body measures (BMX), smoking questionnaire (SMQ), alcohol use (ALQ).

data.name: NHANES Linked Mortality Files (through December 31, 2019)
data.description: Public-use linked mortality files from NCHS providing vital status and follow-up time through December 31, 2019, linked to National Death Index.

data.name: Variable Codebook
data.description: Complete variable definitions, value labels, CDAI calculation methodology, and survey design notes for all NHANES 2007-2018 components used in this analysis.

primary_methods: subgroup analysis by age, smoking, hypertension
metadata.dataset_source: NHANES 2007-2018 (6 combined cycles) with NDI-linked mortality
